The classification of Ervum lathyroides is used to understand its evolutionary history and genetic relationships with other plant species.

intro: Ervum lathyroides, commonly known as sweet cicely or wild celery, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and menstrual cramps. The bioactive constituents of Ervum lathyroides include essential oils, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, sweet cicely is made into herbal teas, syrups, and infusions, which are used to aid digestion, soothe respiratory issues, and calm nervous system imbalances.
